rows.description: Pages served over plain HTTP. HTTPS is a ranking signal and browsers mark HTTP pages as 'not secure'.
rows.how_to_fix: Serve all pages over HTTPS and 301 redirect HTTP to HTTPS.

rows.description: Pages with no links to other internal pages. Search engines have trouble discovering pages that are not linked, and PageRank cannot flow onwards.
rows.how_to_fix: Link to relevant internal pages to help users continue their journey and pass PageRank onwards. If links are only client-side, render them server-side.

rows.description: No XML sitemap found. Sitemaps are the primary discovery mechanism for search engines and many AI crawlers.
rows.how_to_fix: Generate a sitemap.xml of canonical URLs and reference it from robots.txt.

rows.description: Pages with no canonical URL (link element or HTTP header). Without one, search engines pick the version they consider best, which can be unpredictable when multiple versions exist.
rows.how_to_fix: Specify a canonical URL on every indexable page.

rows.description: No robots.txt found. Crawlers (search and AI) fall back to default behavior and you lose the ability to declare sitemaps and per-bot policies.
rows.how_to_fix: Add a robots.txt declaring crawl policy and the sitemap location.

rows.description: Pages whose titles exceed 60 characters. Characters over the limit may be truncated in Google's search results and carry less weight in scoring.
rows.how_to_fix: Write concise page titles so important words are not truncated in the SERPs.

rows.description: Pages without Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image). Shares on social platforms and chat apps render without a rich preview, lowering CTR.
rows.how_to_fix: Add og:title, og:description, og:image and og:url meta tags.

rows.description: No llms.txt found. llms.txt is an emerging standard that gives LLMs and AI agents a curated, markdown map of your most important content - improving how AI systems summarize and cite the site.
rows.how_to_fix: Add /llms.txt with a short site description and links to key pages and docs.

rows.description: No Organization (or WebSite) schema found anywhere on the site. Entity-level markup helps AI systems attribute content to a verified organization (E-E-A-T).
rows.how_to_fix: Add Organization JSON-LD (name, url, logo, sameAs) on the homepage.

rows.description: Pages with multiple <h2>s. HTML standards allow this within a logical heading hierarchy; the filter helps review whether they are used appropriately.
rows.how_to_fix: Ensure <h2>s sit in a logical hierarchical structure, using h3-h6 for deeper levels where appropriate.

rows.description: URLs missing the Content-Security-Policy response header, which guards against cross-site scripting by controlling which resources may load.
rows.how_to_fix: Set a strict Content-Security-Policy response header across all pages.

rows.description: URLs missing the X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header, allowing browsers to MIME-sniff responses away from the declared content type.
rows.how_to_fix: Set X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on all responses.

rows.description: URLs missing X-Frame-Options (or CSP frame-ancestors), leaving pages embeddable in iframes and exposed to clickjacking.
rows.how_to_fix: Set X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN or a CSP frame-ancestors directive.

rows.description: URLs missing a secure Referrer-Policy (no-referrer-when-downgrade, strict-origin-when-cross-origin, no-referrer or strict-origin). Full URLs can leak cross-origin in the Referer header.
rows.how_to_fix: Set Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

rows.description: Pages with a missing, empty or whitespace meta description. This is a missed opportunity to influence click-through rates from search results.
rows.how_to_fix: Write unique, descriptive meta descriptions on key pages that communicate the purpose of the page and entice clicks.

rows.description: Section headings lack id attributes, so deep links to specific passages are impossible. Citable fragments improve how AI answers and SERP features link in.
rows.how_to_fix: Add stable id attributes to h2/h3 headings to enable deep links.

rows.description: Copy on the page is very difficult to read (Flesch reading-ease below 30) and is best understood by university graduates.
rows.how_to_fix: Use shorter sentences and simpler words to improve readability for your audience.

rows.description: Pages without BreadcrumbList schema. Breadcrumbs help search and answer engines understand site hierarchy and display better result paths.
rows.how_to_fix: Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD reflecting the page's position in the site.

rows.description: Pages without a twitter:card meta tag; X/Twitter shares fall back to a bare link.
rows.how_to_fix: Add <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image"> plus title, description and image tags.

rows.description: No RSS/Atom feed discovered. Feeds help aggregators and AI systems track fresh content and surface it quickly.
rows.how_to_fix: Publish an RSS/Atom feed and declare it with <link rel="alternate">.

rows.description: No favicon was discovered. Favicons appear in browser tabs, bookmarks and Google's mobile search results.
rows.how_to_fix: Add a favicon and declare it with a <link rel="icon"> element.

issues.detail: Title is 64 characters: 'Autonomous AI Agency v5 - Your AI-powered workforce | StrikerSam'
Page has no (or an empty) meta description
None of the 13 h2/h3 headings have id attributes
Page links to no other internal pages
No BreadcrumbList schema on the page
Page has no Open Graph tags
Page has no twitter:card meta tag
Missing X-Frame-Options header (and no CSP frame-ancestors)
No Organization or WebSite schema found on any crawled page
